data.title: HSBC to sell UK life unit to Chesnara for $355M
data.description:
HSBC (NYSE:HSBC) agreed to sell its HSBC Life (UK) Limited unit to Chesnara Plc for £260M  (US$355M), Chesnara said in a filing on Thursday.

For HSBC (NYSE:HSBC), the deal continues a string of asset sales as the bank focuses more on Asia, amid a broader restructuring push to cut costs and boost profits. Last month, the bank agreed to sell its custody business in Germany. Last year, it agreed to sell its French life insurance business.

In contrast, Chesnara is consolidating life and pension books in the U.K. and the Netherlands, complemented by business written across the group's businesses in Sweden, the Netherlands, and the U.K., it said.

The deal is expected to generate lifetime cash generation of over £800M for Chesnara, through delivery of future profits and run off of capital requirements over time, the company said in the filing.

HSBC Holdings (HSBC) stock was unchanged in U.S. premarket trading on Thursday.

data.title: Salesforce's recent price increases are 'underappreciated' by investors: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) recently announced price increases are “underappreciated” by investors, Morgan Stanley said on Thursday.

“While price increases targeted on Enterprise/Unlimited tiers represent modest tailwinds to FY27/FY28 [subscription revenue], we see strategic rationale inducing upgrades to Premium tiers as underappreciated,” analyst Keith Weiss wrote in a note to clients. “Pricing & Packaging remains a compelling catalyst for growth acceleration to re-rate shares.” 

Weiss, who has an Overweight rating and $404 price target on Salesforce, made similar comments last month. 

The price increases — which go into effect in August — were likely designed to push adoption of Salesforce's premium tiers and give customers more value for their bundles, Weiss said. 

“Using Sales Cloud as a proxy for potential cost savings of adopting higher tier bundles vs. adopting similar functionality on a standalone basis, we estimate Unlimited Edition to be ~70% more cost-effective when bundled, while the Agentforce 1 tier is a more material ~100%+ more cost-effective relative to Unlimited on a like-for-like functionality basis,” he posited. “As such, we expect [the]latest pricing action to catalyze upgrades to higher tiers for Salesforce's largest customers looking to maximize the value of their Salesforce solutions, particularly given Data Cloud/Agentforce functionality embedded within the Agentforce 1 tier.”

data.title: AST SpaceMobile secures additional $100M financing
data.description:
• AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) on Thursday announced the closing of a $100M equipment financing facility led by Trinity Capital (NASDAQ:TRIN).
  • This non-dilutive financing is designed to support AST SpaceMobile’s accelerated manufacturing and network deployment goals during 2025 and 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Krispy Kreme appoints Raphael Duvivier as CFO
data.description:
• Krispy Kreme (NASDAQ:DNUT) said on Thursday that Raphael Duvivier, President, International of Krispy Kreme, has been named Chief Financial Officer, effective July 11, 2025. 
  • Duvivier succeeds Jeremiah Ashukian, who decided to leave the company to pursue an opportunity with a private company.
  • Since joining Krispy Kreme in 2019, Duvivier has held multiple leadership roles at the company, including segment Chief Financial and Strategy Officer, International, and Chief Development Officer. 
  • He previously held various senior leadership positions in global finance, development, and operations at Restaurant Brands International (QSR). Prior to that, he held leadership positions at Opus Investimentos. 
  • Additionally, Chief Product Officer Alison Holder has been promoted to Chief Brand and Product Officer effective July 11, 2025, assuming responsibility for the company’s global marketing initiatives.

data.title: Alibaba plans to raise HK$12B via exchangeable bond offering
data.description:
Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) plans to raise about HK$12B ($1.53B) through an exchangeable bond offering to boost investments in cloud infrastructure and global commerce operations.

Alibaba proposed to issue about HK$12B aggregate principal amount of Zero Coupon Exchangeable Bonds due 2032 in a private offering to certain non-U.S. persons in offshore transactions outside the U.S.

The bonds link to Alibaba Health Technology, according to the group. Investors can later exchange these bonds for shares in Alibaba Health. The bonds will not bear regular interest.

Alibaba Group holds about 64% of equity interest in Alibaba Health.

Alibaba intends to use the net proceeds from the bond offering for general corporate purposes, including investments to support the development of its cloud infrastructure and international commerce businesses.

data.title: So-Young stock rises on regaining compliance with Nasdaq minimum bid price requirement
data.description:
• So-Young International (NASDAQ:SY) has regained compliance with the minimum bid price of $1.00 per share requirement set forth under the Nasdaq listing rule.
  • So-Young International (NASDAQ:SY) stock up 7% premarket.

data.title: CrowdStrike checks show 'very robust' demand as momentum continues: Wedbush
data.description:
Recent channel checks into CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) showed demand for its cybersecurity products is “very robust” and that momentum is likely to continue into 2026, Wedbush Securities said.

“The company has seen strength driven by continued platform adoption with healthy momentum in Cloud, Identity, and Logscale coupled with better-than-expected trends in Data Protection and Charlotte AI, which saw considerable strength in deal flow in the most recent quarter, and we believe clearly accelerated this July quarter so far,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. “While AI continues to be top of mind for enterprises heading into an important year for AI monetization, we are seeing more module add-ons within CRWD’s core verticals as we are still in the very early innings of this trend playing out.”

Wedbush analysts raised their price target on CrowdStrike to $575 from $525 and reiterated their Outperform rating.

Delving deeper, the analysts said that CrowdStrike's revenue tied to cyber artificial intelligence products is “significantly” above where it was a year ago. That trend is likely to continue to accelerate, thanks to the strength and stickiness of its Falcon platform.

“CRWD remains the gold standard for cybersecurity with the company in the early innings of its multi-year growth story with [net new annual recurring revenue] growth expected to reaccelerate in the [second-half of 2026], and we believe that CRWD remains at the forefront of securing the AI Revolution over the coming years,” the analysts wrote.

data.title: Nippon Steel to raise $5.6B in subordinated loans to fund US Steel takeover
data.description:
Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) said on Wednesday it will seek to raise ¥800B (~$5.6B) through two subordinated loans to partially fund its recent $14.9B acquisition of US Steel and refinance previous loans.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) will use a ¥500B subordinated loan to partially repay a ¥2T bridge loan secured in June for the deal, and a separate ¥300B loan will refinance a previous ¥450B yen subordinated loan, according to Reuters.

The remaining 1.5T of the bridge loan will be financed through a combination of methods, based on an assessment of interest rates, market conditions and other factors, a company spokesperson said.

Following the US Steel deal, Nippon Steel's (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) debt-to-equity ratio rose to ~0.8 from 0.35 as of March 31 due to the bridge loans and a loss on the sale of its stake in a U.S. joint venture with ArcelorMittal, which it decided to sell to help win approval for the US Steel acquisition.

The company aims to reduce the debt-to-equity ratio to ~0.7 by the end of March 2026 through measures such as cash flow from earnings and asset sales, Reuters said.

data.title: STAR FASHION CULTURE prices $8M public offering of class A ordinary shares
data.description:
• STAR FASHION CULTURE (NASDAQ:STFS) announced on Thursday the pricing of its public offering of 20M Class A ordinary shares, $0.00001 par value per share, at a public offering price of $0.40 par value per share.
  • Gross proceeds, before deducting placement agent fees and other offering expenses, are expected to be $8 million.
